comment: Official 2026 World Cup broadcasters per country (sources: Wikipedia 2026 FIFA World Cup broadcasting rights, FourFourTwo, Goal). teams = that country's qualified team(s) linking to team pages.

countries.name: the USA
countries.broadcasters: FOX and FS1 (English), Telemundo and Universo (Spanish), with streaming on FOX One and Peacock
countries.pitch: FOX shows all 104 matches in the US, but FOX One and a cable login cost add up — and you only get English or Spanish feeds. Premium IPTV gives you every match in 4K plus international commentary for a fraction of the price.

countries.name: the UK
countries.broadcasters: BBC (BBC One/Two and iPlayer) and ITV (ITV1/ITV4 and ITVX) — every match free-to-air
countries.pitch: Every match is free on BBC and ITV in the UK — but their streams are geo-locked, so UK fans abroad get blocked, and 4K coverage is limited. Premium IPTV unlocks every match in true 4K from anywhere in the world.

countries.name: Canada
countries.broadcasters: Bell Media — CTV, TSN and RDS (French)
countries.pitch: TSN and RDS carry the World Cup in Canada behind a cable or streaming subscription. Premium IPTV gives Canadian fans every match in 4K — including the host-nation games — without the cable bill.

countries.name: France
countries.broadcasters: M6 (54 matches free-to-air) and beIN Sports (all 104 matches)
countries.pitch: For the first time in nearly 50 years TF1 has no matches — M6 shows just 54 free, and you need a beIN Sports subscription for the rest. Premium IPTV gives you all 104 matches in 4K with French commentary, no beIN subscription required.

countries.name: Belgium
countries.pitch: VRT and RTBF carry the World Cup free in Belgium, but their streams are geo-locked and limited to Dutch or French commentary. Premium IPTV gives you every match in 4K with any commentary, from anywhere.

countries.name: Germany
countries.broadcasters: ARD and ZDF (free) plus Magenta Sport, which holds all 104 matches
countries.pitch: ARD and ZDF only show a share of matches free — Magenta Sport holds all 104 behind a paid Telekom plan. Premium IPTV gives you every match in 4K with German commentary, no Magenta subscription needed.

countries.name: Spain
countries.broadcasters: RTVE (free, selected matches) and DAZN (the full tournament)
countries.pitch: RTVE only airs a selection free — you need DAZN for the complete tournament. Premium IPTV gives you all 104 matches in 4K with Spanish commentary, without a DAZN subscription.

countries.name: Portugal
countries.broadcasters: RTP, SIC, TVI and Sport TV
countries.pitch: Coverage in Portugal is split across RTP, SIC, TVI and Sport TV — and full access usually means a Sport TV subscription. Premium IPTV gives you every match in 4K with Portuguese commentary in one place.

countries.name: the Netherlands
countries.pitch: NOS streams the World Cup free in the Netherlands, but it is geo-locked and limited to Dutch commentary in standard quality. Premium IPTV gives you every match in true 4K from anywhere.

countries.name: Italy
countries.broadcasters: RAI (free, selected matches) and Sky Sport / NOW (the full tournament)
countries.pitch: Italy didn't qualify for 2026 — but you can still follow every match. RAI shows only a selection free and Sky Sport holds the rest. Premium IPTV gives you all 104 matches in 4K with Italian commentary, no Sky subscription.

countries.name: Greece
countries.pitch: ERT carries the World Cup in Greece, but coverage is geo-locked and in standard quality. Even with Greece not competing in 2026, Premium IPTV lets you follow every match in 4K with Greek or international commentary.

countries.name: Albania
countries.pitch: TV Klan and SuperSport show the World Cup in Albania, but full access means a SuperSport subscription. Premium IPTV gives you every match in 4K with Albanian and international commentary in one subscription.

countries.name: Romania
countries.pitch: Antena holds the World Cup in Romania, but its streams are geo-locked and limited in quality. Premium IPTV gives you every match in true 4K from anywhere, with Romanian or international commentary.
